action.reasoning: Observation: The last three iterations (12-14) all failed with a torch.distributed DistNetworkError because torchrun resolved the container hostname instead of localhost for the TCP rendezvous. No training occurred, so our best test macro_f1 remains 0.4197. Analysis: We can bypass torchrun entirely by using torch.multiprocessing.spawn inside train.py with MASTER_ADDR hard-coded to 127.0.0.1. This avoids hostname resolution issues completely. To maximize the score with the remaining ~2.9h, we should (1) warm-start the ViT-L/14 backbone from the existing good checkpoint (checkpoints_v2/best.pt), (2) attach a stronger 2-layer MLP head trained from scratch, (3) use a stable DDP recipe with AMP, cosine warmup, differential LR, label smoothing, gradient clipping, and RandAugment. A 10-epoch run should finish in ~1.5h based on prior timing, leaving room for evaluation. Decision: Rewrite train.py using mp.spawn, add a --pretrained_checkpoint flag to load the old backbone, keep predict.py unchanged, and launch a 10-epoch training run.
